title: Kellanova (K) Economic Prospect Score — Consensus Analysis
description: Consensus economic prospect analysis of Kellanova (K), averaging independent scores from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1 across competitive momentum, moat durability, and sentiment catalysts.
verdictDetail: Kellanova (formerly Kellogg Company) was an American multinational food manufacturing company that owned major snack brands like Pringles, Cheez-It, and Pop-Tarts. As explicitly verified in exploration, the company was acquired by Mars Inc. for nearly $30 billion, with the acquisition completed on December 11, 2025. Because it is no longer an independent, publicly traded entity, it receives an automatic baseline prospect score of 0.

methodology: Consensus Analysis — Economic Prospect Score averaging independent evaluations from Opus 4.6 and Gemini 3.1. Gemini scored K at 0/100 and Opus at 12/100. Each factor score is the arithmetic mean of both models. Three pillars: Competitive Momentum (0-35), Moat Durability (0-35), and Sentiment & Catalysts (0-30).
